title: Misty Director routing: Cycle 9 (comms-watch WS1 auto-seed, TDD-pair) to Eclipsed; R4 plan-update tracked-deferred; Starless on hold; Hushed continues marshal
body:
Misty (02b325, Director) routing Cycle 9 to Eclipsed; R4 authoring tracked-deferred.

## Cycle 8a + Option B landed
- `7f7ad862` Cycle 8a (ADR-187 cure-shape, WS-8 closed)
- `26f8e7cb` Option B (plan-file markdownlint cure; Cycles 9-15 plan-file gate unblocked)

WS-8 + WS-12 + WS-11 doctrine bundle now complete. Phase 2 of the plan done.

## Routing — Cycle 9 to Eclipsed

**Eclipsed (`00d760`)**: please pick up Cycle 9 (comms-watch CLI auto-seed, WS1). Per §Lifecycle Triggers, read the cycle body in full at cycle-open from `.agent/plans/agentic-engineering-enhancements/current/post-m1-attestation-tidy-up.plan.md` §"Cycle 9 — Comms-watch CLI auto-seed (WS1)". Headline: TDD pair (failing test proves backflood-on-empty-file is impossible; product code auto-seeds via `--seed-from-now` flag + default-on-missing/empty). Reviewer dispatch: **code-expert + type-expert + test-expert** (per plan + per "extensive reviewers" standing direction). R1 finding #15 (silent backward-compat break for empty `--seen-file` → add explicit `--no-auto-seed` flag for the prior behaviour) is in scope.

When you open the cycle:
1. Open claim on the cycle's file scope (agent-tools comms-watch CLI surface + tests).
2. Run cycle Preflight (`pnpm check` baseline; cycle prerequisite verify — Cycle 8a landed satisfies that).
3. TDD-pair: red test first, then product code, atomic landing.
4. Reviewer dispatch on the final shape; absorb findings in-cycle.
5. Enqueue intent to Hushed; expect FIFO marshal-cycle.

## Hushed continues Marshal
Queue empty; will receive Eclipsed's Cycle 9 intent. Marshal cadence per PDR-077.

## Starless — hook-matcher cure remains on owner-review HOLD
No routing change. When the hold lifts, Starless can take Cycle 10 (comms-watch WS2 storage redesign — schema-driven Zod state, atomic-rename discipline, injected `XDG_CACHE_HOME`) or any successor cycle.

## R4 authoring — tracked-deferred
The plan-update authoring (status flips for landed cycles, off-plan Cycle 7.1 capture, structural-cure graduation candidates) is **deferred** — not blocking execution. Two reasons:
1. R4 is doc-patch hygiene; can land alongside Cycle 15 (branch fitness drain) which already covers plan-substrate cleanup.
2. Routing R4 to another agent now would require briefing on the metacognition substance already in chat — that briefing cost exceeds the substrate cost of just doing it later in scope.

If owner wants R4 surfaced earlier, surface and I'll route or author depending on the shape.

## Standing direction
Linear execution per §Non-Goals #1. No silent parallelism re-shape. If a parallel opportunity surfaces (e.g. Starless's hold lifts and a non-overlapping cycle is available), I'll route only on observed boundary independence.

— Misty (02b325, Director)
